epigraph.text: Iron sharpens iron, and one man sharpens another.
epigraph.quote: The essence of mathematics is not to make simple things complicated, but to make complicated things simple.

content.text: {The essence of mathematics is not to make simple things complicated, but to make complicated things simple.}{Stan Gudder}

This chapter has given you three methods for solving systems of linear 
equations. Each method has its strengths, and the skilled mathematician 
chooses the most efficient tool for each problem.
Match each term with its description.

items: System of equations A. Lines that never intersect
Solution of a system B. Solving one eq. for a variable and substituting
Independent system C. Two or more equations considered together
Inconsistent system D. Adding/subtracting equations to eliminate a variable
Dependent system E. An ordered pair satisfying all equations
Substitution method F. Equations representing the same line
Elimination method G. System with exactly one solution
Parallel lines H. System with no solution

content.text:
Classify each system as independent, inconsistent, or 
dependent. Then solve if possible.

The sum of two numbers is 84 and their difference is 26. 
Find the numbers.

A cashier has 56 bills, all \$5 and \$10 bills, totaling 
\$410. How many of each denomination are there?

A kayaker paddles 12 miles downstream in 1.5 hours. The 
return trip takes 3 hours. Find the kayaker's paddling speed and 
the current speed.
Choose the most efficient method and solve.
Write a system of equations and solve.

items:
A rectangle's perimeter is 56 cm. Its length is 4 cm more 
 than its width. Find the dimensions.

Concert tickets cost \$25 for general admission and \$40 for 
 reserved seating. 500 tickets were sold for \$15,250. How many 
 of each type were sold?
